entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop grown in eastern and southern Africa

entities.name: EASTERN AND SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where pigeonpea landraces were collected and cultivars were distributed

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Local varieties of pigeonpea used as genetic resources for breeding

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics of pigeonpea landraces evaluated for breeding

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pigeonpea to resist the fusarium wilt disease

entities.name: MARKET-PREFERRED TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics of pigeonpea that are desired by consumers and markets

entities.name: REGIONAL BREEDING PROGRAM
entities.description: A structured effort to improve pigeonpea genetics in the ESA region

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The process of improving the genetic traits of pigeonpea

entities.name: LONG-DURATION CULTIVARS
entities.description: Pigeonpea varieties with a longer growth cycle, developed for high resistance to fusarium wilt and large grain size

entities.name: 100-GRAIN WEIGHT
entities.description: A trait measuring the weight of 100 grains, used to determine grain size

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The trait of reaching maturity quickly, found in medium-duration cultivars

entities.name: MEDIUM-DURATION CULTIVARS
entities.description: Pigeonpea varieties with a medium growth cycle, developed for high latitude areas and ratoon ability

entities.name: RATOONING ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a pigeonpea plant to produce a second crop from the stubble of the first

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop yield per unit area

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The condition of having reliable access to sufficient food, improved by pigeonpea productivity
